parsed_review.summary: CP00-945 is a clean, descriptor-only RP30 P06 pack bridging HRPermission tail rows (RP30.P06.M00.S11-S13) and SensitiveGuard foundation rows (RP30.P06.M01.S01-S07). The pack-level claim is narrow and honest: manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only=true, with the only positive flag being hrx_hr_permission_sensitive_guard_bridge_descriptor_verified. That claim is fully supported by static inspection: (1) the registry binding (registry.js:899-914) and requirements (registry.js:3480-3532) match the upstream plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:392-524), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, baseline, and source microphase ledger (M00=HRPermission, M01=SensitiveGuard); (2) the generated descriptor and domain rows hold every runtime/record-write/decision-emission/real-data/permission-bypass flag false and keep sensitive_hr_guard_required/h30_review_only/c30_read_only/human_final_approval_required true (registry.js:3583-3885, 10604-10679); (3) the coverage/descriptor/contract validators enforce all of these and exit-on-error (validators.js:8362-8547); (4) the committed contract artifact hrx-people-contract.json is consistent with the projection (current_pack/latest_pack CP00-945; SensitiveGuard subphase_7 boundaries all false, entity_name SensitiveGuard); (5) tests (model.test.js:2383-2472) assert binding, distribution counts, descriptor boundaries, and validate the real contract file; (6) the validate script is read-only and chains CP897->CP945. No-write attestation is comprehensive, no real/credential data appears, and Hermes is used only as a deterministic control-plane gate/review packet (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. The authority boundary is respected: Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver, and the pack does not assert runtime or enterprise-trust, so blocks_goal_closeout is false. The PENDING state of adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and claude-review-result.json is the expected pre-review state, not a defect. Provenance note: test/validation green status is taken from command-evidence.json exit codes (all 0) plus my static reading of internally consistent code and artifacts; as a read-only reviewer I did not independently re-execute the validation ladder. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 transparency note on validation-ladder evidence.

findings.title: Broader closeout validation ladder not yet captured in command-evidence.json
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only 5 HRX-scoped commands (node --check x3, node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, npm run rp30:hrx:validate), all exit_code 0, with status 'pending_final_validation' and hermes_gate 'pending'. The 10 closeout validation commands listed in the review prompt (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run spec:requirements:validate, npm run weighted:validate, npm run fullplan:validate, npm run goal:closeout:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) are not yet evidenced here. This is the expected pre-closeout state, and as a read-only reviewer I cannot execute them; the descriptor-only claim itself is independently supported by the consistent registry/validators/contract/tests inspected. Flagged only for provenance transparency.
findings.recommendation: Closeout runner should execute and capture the full validation ladder (including git diff --check) with exit 0 before flipping manifest and included_units status from in_progress to closed and writing the review receipt; ensure command-evidence/Hermes gate reflect the completed ladder rather than 'pending'.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; expected pre-closeout state. Deferred to the pack closeout runner at final validation.
